---
title: "Chapter 16: Using Ratings by Trained Observers"
description: "Chapter-level study notes for Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "handbook-practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Chapter"
chapter_number: 16
source_pages: "454-486"
citation_key: "newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch16-chapter-16-using-ratings-by-trained-observers:c0001"
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-2:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

# Chapter 16: Using Ratings by Trained Observers

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>454-486</span><span>Part 2: Practical Data Collection Procedures</span><span>Source status: needs_review</span></div>

## Core Claim

Trained observer ratings turn direct observation into systematic evidence through explicit scales and procedures.

## Summary

The chapter covers ratings of physical conditions, interactions, facilities, and service environments.

Anchored scales and trained raters can generate comparable data, but reliability depends on training, calibration, and quality control.

Observer ratings are especially useful when conditions can be seen directly but are not captured well in administrative records or surveys.

## Key Concepts

- **trained observer rating**: Systematic rating by observers using explicit scales or criteria.
- **data collection plan**: The operational plan linking evaluation questions to information sources, instruments, fieldwork, and staffing.
- **performance measurement**: Ongoing monitoring of indicators to support accountability and improvement.

## Practice Implications

- Use explicit rating anchors and training protocols.
- Check inter-rater consistency.
- Match observation schedules to the program conditions being assessed.

## Source Records

- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch16-chapter-16-using-ratings-by-trained-observers:c0001`
- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-2:c0001`

## References

These notes summarise source-linked records from *Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation* [@newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation].
